briefing: Warns about risks of rapid agentic AI rollouts, citing concerns from Five Eyes intelligence agencies.

articles.title: Google Pixel vs. Samsung Galaxy: I've tested both brands extensively, and there's a clear winner

articles.title: Moving to mainframe can be cheaper than sticking with VMware: Gartner
articles.snippet: Serious Linux VMs will enjoy big iron – if you can learn to love lock-in risks and skills challenges VMware users considering a new home might find it cheaper to move to an IBM mainframe than adopting Broadcom’s new licenses, according to Gartner Vice President Analyst Alessandro Galimberti.…
articles.ai_summary: Gartner suggests that moving to IBM mainframes may be more cost-effective for serious Linux VM users than sticking with VMware due to new licensing issues. This shift could reshape how organizations manage their infrastructure.
articles.why_it_matters: Reveals potential cost-saving strategies for businesses considering infrastructure changes.

articles.title: Google Maps vs. Waze: I've driven with the two best navigation apps, and one is much better

articles.title: If the vote you rocked, your personal info can be grokked
articles.snippet: Even limited voter rolls can be linked to identify people, research shows Your voter data could be used against you. A foreign intelligence service that wished to identify the family members of deployed military personnel could do so by cross-referencing public voter record data and social media posts.…
articles.ai_summary: Research indicates that even limited voter rolls can be exploited to identify individuals, raising concerns about privacy and security. This highlights the risks associated with public voter data in the wrong hands.
articles.why_it_matters: Emphasizes the importance of protecting personal data from potential misuse.

articles.title: Five Eyes spook shops warn rapid rollouts of agentic AI are too risky
articles.snippet: Prioritize resilience over productivity, say CISA, NCSC and their friends from Oz, NZ, Canada Information security agencies from the nations of the Five Eyes security alliance have co-authored guidance on the use of agentic AI that warns the technology will likely misbehave and amplifies organizations’ existing frailties, and therefore recommend slow and careful adoption of the tech.…
articles.ai_summary: The Five Eyes intelligence alliance warns against the rapid deployment of agentic AI, citing risks of misbehavior and existing vulnerabilities in organizations. They recommend a cautious approach to adopting this technology.
articles.why_it_matters: Calls for careful consideration of AI implementation to avoid security pitfalls.
